Summary: BUG-006: Equipment Detail Mismatch
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Equipment specifications displayed in UI do not match master database records.

h3. Description

Several equipment records display incorrect serial number and capacity information.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Search equipment ID EQ-45872.
# Open Equipment Details page.
# Compare displayed data with Master Database.

h3. Expected Result

Equipment details should match master records.

h3. Actual Result

Incorrect capacity and serial number shown.

h3. Business Impact

Incorrect equipment information may affect operational decisions.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

Equipment Management

Summary: BUG-005: Formula Calculation Mismatch
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Energy consumption formula returns incorrect calculation results.

h3. Description

Application-calculated energy efficiency value differs from expected business formula by approximately 12%.

h3. Formula

Expected:
Efficiency = Output Power / Input Power × 100

h3. Observed

System applies incorrect multiplier causing inaccurate results.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Enter sample values.
# Execute calculation.
# Compare with expected result.

h3. Expected Result

Calculation should match approved business formula.

h3. Actual Result

Calculated value differs significantly.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Business Logic Engine

Summary: BUG-004: Multi-Language Translation Display Issue
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Japanese language labels are displayed incorrectly on Equipment Registration page.

h3. Description

Several field labels appear in English while application language is configured as Japanese.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Login to application.
# Change language to Japanese.
# Navigate to Equipment Registration.
# Verify field labels.

h3. Expected Result

All labels should be translated into Japanese.

h3. Actual Result

Mixed English and Japanese labels displayed.

h3. Impact

User confusion and localization compliance issue.

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

Localization / UI

Summary: BUG-003: Firewall Restriction Preventing Cross-Server Communication
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Application server unable to establish communication with external reporting server.

h3. Description

Users are unable to generate reports because requests from Application Server to Reporting Server are blocked.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Login to application.
# Open Reports Module.
# Generate monthly report.
# Observe error.

h3. Expected Result

Report should be generated successfully.

h3. Actual Result

Communication timeout error displayed.

h3. Technical Observation

Port 8443 connection blocked by firewall policy.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Infrastructure / Network

Summary: BUG-002: Database Synchronization Failure
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Customer master data synchronization fails between Application Server and Central Database.

h3. Description

Newly created customer records are not being synchronized to the reporting database resulting in inconsistent data across systems.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Create a new customer record.
# Verify successful save.
# Check Reporting Database.
# Verify synchronization status.

h3. Expected Result

Customer data should synchronize within 5 minutes.

h3. Actual Result

Records remain unavailable in Reporting Database.

h3. Error Log

Connection timeout during synchronization job execution.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Data Synchronization

Summary: BUG-001: User Authority Verification Failure
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Unauthorized users are able to access restricted administrative functions despite lacking required permissions.

h3. Description

During validation of role-based access control, users assigned with "Viewer" role were able to access the User Management page and perform edit operations.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Login as Viewer role user.
# Navigate to Administration → User Management.
# Edit an existing user profile.
# Save changes.

h3. Expected Result

Viewer users should have read-only access and should not be able to modify user information.

h3. Actual Result

System allows user modifications without validating authority.

h3. Environment

* QA Environment
* Build 5.2.1

h3. Severity

Critical

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Authorization / Security

Summary: Sprint 0 - Testing
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
*Summary:*
Perform comprehensive end-to-end functional testing of the application covering user authorization, data synchronization, system integrations, multilingual support, business rule validations, and equipment management functionalities.

*Description:*
Execute end-to-end testing to validate the application's core business functions and ensure system stability, data integrity, security compliance, and seamless integration across all modules.

*Scope:*

* Verify user authentication and role-based authorization controls.
* Validate data synchronization between application and backend databases.
* Verify communication between application servers and integrated systems.
* Validate multilingual support and localization functionality.
* Verify business formulas, calculations, and rule implementations.
* Validate equipment master data accuracy and consistency.
* Perform regression testing on impacted modules.
* Verify error handling, logging, and exception scenarios.
* Validate UI, functional, and integration workflows.

*Test Coverage:*

# User Authority Verification
# Database Synchronization Validation
# Cross-Server Connectivity Validation
# Multi-Language Support Testing
# Formula & Business Rule Validation
# Equipment Information Validation

*Expected Deliverables:*

* Test Execution Report
* Defect Log and Analysis Report
* Regression Summary
* Risk Assessment Report
* Release Readiness Recommendation

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* All critical and major test scenarios executed successfully.
* No open critical defects.
* Data integrity maintained across integrated systems.
* Business calculations match approved specifications.
* User authorization controls function as per requirements.
* Application ready for release recommendation based on test results.

*Priority:* High
*Type:* Testing Task
*Estimated Effort:* 5 Person-Days
*Assignee:* QC Team
*Status:* To Do

Summary: BUG-007: Session Token Not Invalidated After Logout
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

User session remains active even after clicking the Logout button.

h3. Description

After logging out, the previous session token can still be used to access authenticated API endpoints and pages via browser back navigation.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Login to the application.
# Navigate to any authenticated page.
# Click Logout.
# Press the browser Back button or replay a captured API request using the old session token.

h3. Expected Result

Session token should be invalidated on the server immediately after logout, and any subsequent request should redirect to the login page.

h3. Actual Result

Authenticated pages and API calls remain accessible using the old session token.

h3. Severity

Critical

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Authentication / Security

Summary: BUG-008: Password Reset Link Does Not Expire
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Password reset link remains valid indefinitely instead of expiring after the documented time window.

h3. Description

The "Forgot Password" email link is expected to expire 30 minutes after generation, but the link continues to work even after several days.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Trigger a password reset from the login page.
# Wait more than 30 minutes.
# Open the reset link from the email.
# Set a new password.

h3. Expected Result

Reset link should be rejected and display an "expired link" message after 30 minutes.

h3. Actual Result

Reset link allows password change regardless of how much time has passed.

h3. Severity

Critical

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Authentication / Security

Summary: BUG-009: File Upload Accepts Unsupported File Types
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Document upload control allows executable and script files despite UI restrictions.

h3. Description

The Attachments section is intended to allow only PDF, DOCX, and image files, but the upload accepts .exe and .js files without any validation error.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Open any record with an Attachments section.
# Click Upload File.
# Select a file with .exe extension.
# Submit the upload.

h3. Expected Result

Upload should be rejected with a message listing the allowed file types.

h3. Actual Result

File is uploaded and stored successfully regardless of extension.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

File Management

Summary: BUG-010: Pagination Displays Duplicate Records on Subsequent Pages
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

List view pagination shows the same records repeated across multiple pages.

h3. Description

When navigating through paginated results on the Customer List page, several records from page 1 reappear on page 2.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to Customer List.
# Set page size to 20.
# Go to page 1 and note the last 5 records.
# Go to page 2.
# Compare the first 5 records with the last 5 records of page 1.

h3. Expected Result

Each record should appear only once across all pages.

h3. Actual Result

Several records are duplicated between page 1 and page 2.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

UI / Data Display

Summary: BUG-011: Search Returns No Results for Partial Keyword Match
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Global search fails to return results when a partial keyword is entered.

h3. Description

Searching for "Equip" does not return records containing "Equipment", even though partial match search is a documented requirement.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Open the global Search bar.
# Enter a partial keyword, e.g. "Equip".
# Press Enter.

h3. Expected Result

Search should return all records whose fields contain the entered substring.

h3. Actual Result

Search returns "No results found" for partial keywords; only exact matches return results.

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

Search

Summary: BUG-012: Email Notification Not Triggered on Status Change
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Users are not notified by email when the status of their assigned ticket changes.

h3. Description

Per the notification configuration, an email should be sent to the assignee whenever a ticket status changes from "Open" to "Resolved". This email is not being sent.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Open a ticket assigned to a test user.
# Change the status from Open to Resolved.
# Save the change.
# Check the assignee's mailbox.

h3. Expected Result

Assignee should receive an email notification confirming the status change.

h3. Actual Result

No email notification is received.

h3. Error Log

Notification queue shows job completed with zero recipients.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Notification Engine

Summary: BUG-013: Date Field Displays Incorrect Timezone Offset
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Timestamps on the Activity Log are displayed in UTC instead of the user's local timezone.

h3. Description

The Activity Log shows event times that are offset by several hours compared to the user's configured timezone in profile settings.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Set user profile timezone to "Asia/Tokyo".
# Perform an action that creates an activity log entry.
# Open the Activity Log.
# Compare the displayed timestamp with the system clock.

h3. Expected Result

Timestamps should be converted and displayed according to the user's configured timezone.

h3. Actual Result

Timestamps are displayed in UTC regardless of profile timezone setting.

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

Localization / UI

Summary: BUG-014: Export to Excel Generates Corrupted File for Large Datasets
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Exporting more than 10,000 records to Excel produces a file that cannot be opened.

h3. Description

When exporting the full Equipment list (approximately 15,000 records) to Excel, the downloaded .xlsx file fails to open in Microsoft Excel with a "file format or extension is not valid" error.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to Equipment List.
# Remove all filters so that all ~15,000 records are in scope.
# Click Export to Excel.
# Open the downloaded file in Microsoft Excel.

h3. Expected Result

Exported file should open correctly and contain all records, optionally split across multiple sheets.

h3. Actual Result

Excel reports the file is corrupted and cannot be opened.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Reporting / Export

Summary: BUG-015: Form Validation Allows Special Characters in Name Field
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

The Customer Name field accepts special characters that should be blocked by validation.

h3. Description

The First Name and Last Name fields on the Customer Registration form are documented to allow only letters, spaces, and hyphens, but currently accept characters such as < > { } ; and emoji.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to Customer Registration.
# Enter "<script>" in the First Name field.
# Complete the remaining required fields.
# Submit the form.

h3. Expected Result

Form should display a validation error and prevent submission when invalid characters are entered.

h3. Actual Result

Form submits successfully and stores the value as entered.

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Low

h3. Module

Input Validation

Summary: BUG-016: Concurrent Edits Cause Silent Data Overwrite
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

When two users edit the same record simultaneously, the second save silently overwrites the first user's changes.

h3. Description

There is no optimistic locking or conflict warning when two users open the same Equipment record for editing and save changes within a short time of each other.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# User A opens Equipment record EQ-12345 for editing.
# User B opens the same record for editing.
# User A updates the Capacity field and saves.
# User B updates the Location field and saves.

h3. Expected Result

System should warn User B that the record was modified since it was loaded and prompt to merge or reload.

h3. Actual Result

User B's save overwrites User A's change to the Capacity field without any warning.

h3. Severity

Critical

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Data Integrity

Summary: BUG-017: Session Timeout Does Not Redirect User to Login Page
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

After the session expires due to inactivity, the application does not redirect the user to the login page.

h3. Description

Once the configured 30-minute idle timeout is reached, subsequent navigation within the application shows blank pages or partial data instead of redirecting to the login screen.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Login to the application.
# Leave the browser tab idle for more than 30 minutes.
# Click on any navigation menu item.

h3. Expected Result

User should be redirected to the login page with a "session expired" message.

h3. Actual Result

Page attempts to load and shows empty content or a generic error without redirecting to login.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

Session Management

Summary: BUG-018: Column Sort Order Not Persisted After Page Refresh
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Column sort order selected by the user resets after refreshing the page.

h3. Description

On the Tickets List page, sorting by "Priority" column is not retained after the browser is refreshed; the list reverts to the default sort order.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to Tickets List.
# Click on the "Priority" column header to sort descending.
# Refresh the browser page.

h3. Expected Result

The list should remain sorted by "Priority" descending after refresh.

h3. Actual Result

List reverts to the default sort order (Created date descending).

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Low

h3. Module

UI / Usability

Summary: BUG-019: API Response Time Exceeds SLA Under Peak Load
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

The /api/equipment/search endpoint exceeds the 2-second SLA response time during peak usage hours.

h3. Description

During load testing simulating 200 concurrent users, the equipment search API has an average response time of 6.5 seconds, well above the documented 2-second SLA.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Run the load test script with 200 concurrent virtual users against /api/equipment/search.
# Monitor average and 95th percentile response times.

h3. Expected Result

95th percentile response time should remain under 2 seconds under the tested load.

h3. Actual Result

Average response time is 6.5 seconds and 95th percentile exceeds 12 seconds.

h3. Technical Observation

Database query for the search endpoint is missing an index on the "equipment_status" column.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Performance / API

Summary: BUG-020: Profile Image Thumbnail Not Rendered After Upload
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Uploaded profile picture does not appear as a thumbnail on the user's profile page.

h3. Description

After successfully uploading a new profile picture, the profile page continues to show the default avatar icon instead of the uploaded image.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to My Profile.
# Click Change Picture and upload a JPEG image.
# Wait for the success message.
# Refresh the profile page.

h3. Expected Result

The newly uploaded image should be displayed as the profile thumbnail.

h3. Actual Result

Default avatar icon continues to be displayed; thumbnail is not updated.

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

UI / Media

Summary: BUG-021: Duplicate Records Created on Double-Click Form Submission
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Rapidly double-clicking the Save button on the New Customer form creates two identical customer records.

h3. Description

The Save button is not disabled after the first click, allowing a second click before the page navigates away, which results in duplicate records being submitted to the server.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to New Customer form.
# Fill in all required fields.
# Quickly double-click the Save button.
# Search for the newly created customer.

h3. Expected Result

Only one customer record should be created, and the Save button should be disabled after the first click.

h3. Actual Result

Two identical customer records are created with different Issue ids.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Form Handling

Summary: BUG-022: Dashboard Layout Breaks on Mobile Viewport
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Dashboard widgets overlap and become unreadable on mobile screen sizes.

h3. Description

When the application is accessed on a mobile device or a browser window narrower than 480px, the dashboard widgets overlap each other and the navigation menu covers part of the content.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Open the application dashboard in a browser.
# Resize the browser window to 375px width (or open on a mobile device).
# Observe the layout of the dashboard widgets and navigation menu.

h3. Expected Result

Dashboard should reflow into a single-column responsive layout with no overlapping elements.

h3. Actual Result

Widgets overlap and navigation menu obscures dashboard content.

h3. Severity

Major

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

Responsive Design / UI

Summary: BUG-023: Generic Error Message Shown for Invalid Login Credentials
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Login page displays a generic "Something went wrong" message instead of a clear invalid credentials message.

h3. Description

When a user enters an incorrect password, the login page shows a generic server error message rather than informing the user that the username or password is incorrect.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to the Login page.
# Enter a valid username with an incorrect password.
# Click Login.

h3. Expected Result

A clear message such as "Invalid username or password" should be displayed.

h3. Actual Result

A generic "Something went wrong, please try again later" message is displayed, causing user confusion.

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Low

h3. Module

Authentication / UX

Summary: BUG-024: Audit Log Does Not Record Delete Operations
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Deleting a record does not create a corresponding entry in the Audit Log.

h3. Description

The Audit Log is expected to capture create, update, and delete operations for compliance purposes. Delete operations on Customer and Equipment records are not being logged.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to Equipment List.
# Delete an existing equipment record.
# Navigate to Audit Log.
# Search for entries related to the deleted record.

h3. Expected Result

Audit Log should contain an entry showing the user, timestamp, and details of the delete operation.

h3. Actual Result

No audit entry is created for the delete operation.

h3. Severity

Critical

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Audit / Compliance

Summary: BUG-025: Stale Data Displayed After Browser Back Navigation
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Using the browser Back button after submitting a form shows outdated data instead of the updated record.

h3. Description

After updating a Customer record and being redirected to the Customer Details page, pressing the browser Back button returns to the edit form pre-filled with the old (pre-update) values, and resubmitting reverts the changes.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Open a Customer record and click Edit.
# Change the Phone Number field and save.
# Verify the Customer Details page shows the new phone number.
# Click the browser Back button.
# Click Save again without changing anything.

h3. Expected Result

Back navigation should either show cached old data clearly marked as stale or force a reload; resubmitting should not silently revert the previous update.

h3. Actual Result

The phone number reverts to the old value after the second save.

h3. Severity

Minor

h3. Priority

Medium

h3. Module

Caching / Navigation

Summary: BUG-026: Invoice Total Rounding Produces Incorrect Currency Values
Creator: Shinu n
Description:
h3. Summary

Invoice total amounts are rounded incorrectly, causing a one-cent discrepancy on multi-line invoices.

h3. Description

When an invoice contains line items with fractional unit prices, the displayed total does not match the sum of the individually rounded line totals, resulting in a one-cent mismatch flagged by the finance team during reconciliation.

h3. Formula

Expected:
Invoice Total = Round(Sum(Line Item Totals), 2)

h3. Observed

System rounds each line item to 2 decimals before summing, but then re-rounds the running subtotal at each step, producing accumulated rounding error.

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Create an invoice with 5 line items, each with a unit price ending in .005.
# Set quantity to 3 for each line item.
# Save the invoice and view the total.
# Manually calculate the expected total.

h3. Expected Result

Invoice total should match the sum of line totals exactly to the cent.

h3. Actual Result

Invoice total is off by $0.01 compared to the manually calculated expected total.

h3. Severity

Critical

h3. Priority

High

h3. Module

Business Logic Engine
